segments.text: "For three years Aram and Israel were not at war.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: "But in the third year Jehoshaphat, king of Judah, went to visit the king of Israel.
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: "The king of Israel had said to his officers,
segments.speaker: Naboth

segments.text: Aren't you aware that Ramoth-gilead really belongs to us and yet we haven't done anything to take it back from the king of Aram?
segments.speaker: The king of Israel had

segments.text: "So he asked Jehoshaphat,
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: Will you join me in an attack to recapture Ramoth-gilead?
segments.speaker: Jehoshaphat

segments.text: Jehoshaphat replied to the king of Israel,
You and I are as one, my men and your men are as one, and my horses and your horses are as one.
segments.speaker: Jehoshaphat

segments.text: "Then Jehoshaphat said to the king of Israel,
segments.speaker: Jezebel

segments.text: But first though, please find out what the Lord says.
segments.speaker: Jezebel

segments.text: "So the king of Israel brought out the prophets—four hundred of them—and he asked them,
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: Should I go up and attack Ramoth-gilead, or should I not?
segments.speaker: Jehoshaphat

segments.text: Yes, go ahead,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they replied,
for the Lord will hand it over to the king.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: "But Jehoshaphat asked,
segments.speaker: Jehoshaphat

segments.text: Isn't there another prophet of the Lord here that we can ask?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Yes, there's another man who could consult the Lord,
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: the king of Israel replied,
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: but I don't like him because he never prophesies anything good for me—it's always bad! His name is Micaiah, son of Imlah.
segments.speaker: Jehoshaphat

segments.text: You shouldn't talk like that,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: said Jehoshaphat.
"The king of Israel called over one of his officials and told him,
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: Bring me Micaiah, son of Imlah, right away.
segments.speaker: The king of Israel

segments.text: "Dressed in their royal robes, the king of Israel and King Jehoshaphat of Judah, were sitting on their thrones at the threshing floor beside the gate of Samaria, with all of the prophets prophesying in front of them.
"One of them, Zedekiah, son of Chenaanah, had made himself iron horns. He announced,
segments.speaker: Zedekiah

segments.text: This is what the Lord says: ‘With these horns you will gore the Arameans until they're dead!’
segments.speaker: The king of Israel

segments.text: "All the prophets were prophesying the same thing, saying,
Go ahead, attack Ramoth-gilead; you will be successful, for the Lord will hand it over to the king.
segments.speaker: The king of Israel

segments.text: "The messenger who went to call Micaiah told him,
segments.speaker: Messenger

segments.text: Look, all the prophets are unanimous in prophesying positively to the king. So please make sure to speak positively like them.
segments.speaker: The king of Israel

segments.text: "But Micaiah replied,
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: As the Lord lives, I can only say what my God tells me.
segments.speaker: The king of Israel

segments.text: "When he came before the king, the king asked him,
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: Should we go up and attack Ramoth-gilead, or should we not?
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: Yes, go ahead and be victorious,
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: Micaiah replied,
for the Lord will give it into the king's hand.
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: "But the king said to him,
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: How many times must I make you swear to tell me only the truth in the name of the Lord?
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: "So Micaiah answered,
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: I saw all Israel scattered on the mountains like sheep without a shepherd. The Lord said, ‘These people have no master; [^64] let each of them go home in peace.’
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: "The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat,
Didn't I tell you he never prophesies anything good for me, only bad?
segments.speaker: The king of Israel

segments.text: "Micaiah went on to say,
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: So listen to what the Lord says. I saw the Lord sitting on his throne, surrounded by the whole army of heaven standing to his right and to his left.
segments.speaker: The king of Israel

segments.text: The Lord asked, ‘Who will trick Ahab, king of Israel, into attacking Ramoth-gilead so he will be killed there?’ One said this, another said that, and another said something else.
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: Finally a spirit came and approached the Lord and said, ‘I will trick him.’
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: ‘How are you going to do that?’ the Lord asked. ‘I will go and be a lying spirit and make all his prophets tell lies,’ the spirit replied. ‘That will work,’ the Lord responded. ‘Go and do it.’
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: As you see, the Lord has put a lying spirit into these prophets of yours, and the Lord has pronounced your death sentence.
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: "Then Zedekiah, son of Chenaanah, went and slapped Micaiah in the face, and demanded,
segments.speaker: Zedekiah

segments.text: Which way did the Spirit of the Lord go when he left me to speak to you?
segments.speaker: The king of Israel

segments.text: You'll soon find out when you try and find some secret place to hide!
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: Micaiah replied.
"The king of Israel ordered,
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: Place Micaiah under arrest and take him back to Amon the governor of the city and to my son Joash.
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: Tell them these are the king's instructions: ‘Put this man in jail. Give him only bread and water until my safe return.’
segments.speaker: The king of Israel

segments.text: If you do in fact return safely then the Lord has not spoken through me,
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: Micaiah declared.
Pay attention everyone to all I've said!
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: "The king of Israel and Jehoshaphat, king of Judah, went to attack Ramoth-gilead.
"The king of Israel told Jehoshaphat,
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: When I go into battle I will be in disguise, but you should wear your royal robes.
segments.speaker: The king of Israel

segments.text: So the king of Israel disguised himself and went into battle.
"The king of Aram had already given these orders to his chariot commanders:
segments.speaker: Aramean king

segments.text: Head straight for the king of Israel alone. Don't fight with anyone else, whoever they are.
segments.speaker: The king of Israel

segments.text: "So when the chariot commanders saw Jehoshaphat, they shouted,
This must be the king of Israel!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So they turned to attack him, but when Jehoshaphat called out for help,
"the chariot commanders saw it wasn't the king of Israel and stopped chasing him.
"However, an enemy archer shot an arrow at random, hitting the king of Israel between the joints of his armor by his breastplate. The king told his charioteer,
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: Turn around and get me out of the fight, because I've been wounded!
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: "The battle lasted all day. The king of Israel was propped up in his chariot to face the Arameans, but in the evening he died. The blood had poured out of his wound onto the floor of the chariot.
"At sunset, a shout went out from the lines:
Retreat! Every man back to his town, every man back to his own country!
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: "So the king died. He was taken back to Samaria where they buried him.
"They washed his chariot at a pool in Samaria where the prostitutes came to bathe, and dogs licked up his blood, just as the Lord had said.
"The rest of what happened in Ahab's reign, all that he did, the ivory palace he constructed and all the cities he built are recorded in the Book of Chronicles of the Kings of Israel.
"Ahab died and his son Ahaziah succeeded him as king.
"Jehoshaphat, son of Asa, became king of Judah in the fourth year of the reign of Ahab, king of Israel.
"Jehoshaphat was thirty-five when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem for twenty-five years. His mother's name was Azubah, daughter of Shilhi.

"He followed all the ways of his father; he did not depart from them, and he did what was right in the Lord's sight. However, the high places were not removed and the people still sacrificed and presented offerings there.
"Jehoshaphat also made peace with the king of Israel.
"The rest of what happened in Jehoshaphat's reign, his great achievements and the wars he fought are recorded in the Book of Chronicles of the Kings of Judah.
"He expelled from the land any cult prostitutes who were left from the time of his father Asa.
"(At that time there was no king in Edom; only a deputy who served as king.)
"Jehoshaphat built seagoing ships[^65] to sail to Ophir for gold, but they didn't go because they were wrecked at Ezion-geber.
"During that time Ahaziah, son of Ahab, asked Jehoshaphat,
segments.speaker: Ahaziah

segments.text: Let my men sail with your men,
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: but Jehoshaphat refused.
"Jehoshaphat died and was buried with his forefathers in the City of David. His son Jehoram succeeded him as king.
"Ahaziah, son of Ahab, became king of Israel in Samaria in the seventeenth year of Jehoshaphat, king of Judah, and he reigned over Israel for two years.
"He did what was evil in the Lord's sight and followed the ways of his father and mother, and of Jeroboam, son of Nebat, who had made Israel sin.
"He served Baal and worshiped him, and angered the Lord, the God of Israel, just as his father had.
